contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped real-time SSE delivery substrate, notification preferences, delivery audit, and platform push operations
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-40 uses client-push as the canonical portability, UI, package, and runtime implementation slug.
coreGuarantees: Connection registration validates tenant and correlation context before pool registration.
SSE pool lookups and event delivery remain tenant-scoped.
Cross-tenant delivery attempts fail before payload dispatch.
Missing connections are discarded without retry queues.
Keepalive cadence is config-driven and dropped connections are cleaned before further emit.
Dropped connections are informational, not error conditions.
Provider and transport access remain behind fabric interfaces.
Tenant credentials are resolved from Vault and are never package material.
Role-specific UI surfaces expose user preferences, tenant admin controls, platform ops, and read-only support trace without internal flow codes.

roleSurface.notes: Notification content is authored by host flows, while FLOW-40 owns preferences, delivery infrastructure, delivery trace, and push substrate behavior.

machineLockedRules: Authentication and correlation validation happen before pool registration.
Connection audit is written after pool registration and remains tenant scoped.
Expired correlation IDs return failure, not exceptions.
FlowEventBridge never emits into user-facing source domains.
Missing connections are discarded without retry.
Keepalive interval and dropped threshold are config inputs, not inline constants.
Dropped connections are closed before any further push attempt.
Dropped connection cleanup logs at info level and does not throw.

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-40 values may tune labels, retention, keepalive cadence, and support trace visibility without changing tenant isolation, auth-before-registration, no-retry missing-connection policy, or cleanup-before-emit behavior.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to push module naming, delivery labels, audit retention, keepalive thresholds, and support trace presentation.

edgeCases.name: Acme client push lab
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes FLOW-40 for compact internal delivery telemetry and shorter retention.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: module labels, retention, and keepalive cadence
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Tenant isolation and missing-connection no-retry policy remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind delivery signal hub
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes FLOW-40 for higher-volume delivery history and tenant-admin friendly labels.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: delivery labels, retention, support visibility, and package metadata
edgeCases.currentImplementation: FlowEventBridge remains read-only toward user-facing source domains.

edgeCases.name: Tessera notification ops loop
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes FLOW-40 for support-focused delivery investigation with summarized traces.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: support trace display, keepalive thresholds, and module naming
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Dropped cleanup still happens before emit and remains info-only.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T587 through T589 remain fixed for the runtime implementation bundle.
Provider and transport access remain behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Authentication and correlation validation happen before connection registration.
SSE connection pool operations remain tenant scoped.
FlowEventBridge does not emit into user-facing source domains.
Missing connections are discarded without retry queues.
Dropped connections are cleaned before any further push attempt.
Dropped connections are logged as informational only.
Marketplace forks must use tenant credentials from Vault.
GitHub Actions and local registry readback are required cascade evidence.

elements: marketplace API as the only valid fork origin

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-40 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-40 package assembly copies the client-push implementation under the canonical package slug path.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
GitHub repo evidence screenshots and local registry publish/readback must exist at each cascade point.
